definitions.es: Una conjunción utilizada para introducir un contraste o excepción.
definitions.vi: Một liên từ được sử dụng để giới thiệu sự tương phản hoặc ngoại lệ.
definitions.en: A conjunction used to introduce a contrast or exception.

example.vi: Tôi thích ăn phở, nhưng mà hôm nay tôi muốn ăn sushi.
example.es: Me gusta comer pho, pero hoy quiero comer sushi.
example.en: I like to eat pho, but today I want to eat sushi.

exampleSentences: Em muốn đi xem phim, nhưng mà hôm nay trời mưa to quá.
Anh thích món ăn này, nhưng mà nó hơi cay đối với anh.
Chị đã cố gắng dậy sớm, nhưng mà vẫn bị trễ học.
notes_md:
**nhưng mà** = **but**. It connects two opposite or contrasting ideas.

It links two full clauses and usually sits in the middle of the sentence.  

It feels a bit more spoken and natural than just **nhưng**.  
Think of **nhưng** as slightly shorter/more neutral, and **nhưng mà** as softer and more conversational.

content.sentence: Tôi thích ăn, ___ không có thời gian.

translation.en: I like to eat, but I have no time.
I want to go, but it rains.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I want to go to the movies, but it is raining heavily today.
I like this dish, but it is a bit too spicy for me.
I tried to wake up early, but I was still late for school.
notes:
nhưng mà = but. It connects two opposite or contrasting ideas.

It links two full clauses and usually sits in the middle of the sentence.  

It feels a bit more spoken and natural than just nhưng.  
Think of nhưng as slightly shorter/more neutral, and nhưng mà as softer and more conversational.
